title: Post char limit compares bytes not characters
description: In posting.rs create_status (line 987) and edit_status (line 1516), the check 'text.len() > state.config.limits.max_post_chars' compares byte length against a config field named max_post_chars. For ASCII this is equivalent, but for multi-byte UTF-8 content (CJK, emoji, etc.), a 500-character post may be 1500+ bytes and get rejected. The Mastodon API specifies character limits in Unicode codepoints. The instance metadata endpoint advertises this as 'max_characters'. Fix: use text.chars().count() instead of text.len(). This also affects the same check in the scheduled post path.
